## Issue: Rename importer binary to kapow

Rename the tools/importer binary from kapow-import to kapow.

Update:
- main.go usage/help text
- go.mod module name if needed
- Any references in docs/plans/kapow-o0j-data-import-tool.md

CLI structure becomes:
  kapow fetch <source>      # download + archive
  kapow import <source>     # import from archive into DB
  kapow run-all             # fetch all + import all in dependency order
  kapow fetch all           # download everything
  kapow import all          # import all in dependency order

6. When done, merge your branch into main:
   git checkout main && git merge worktree/teammate-dtg
7. After merge, close the issue: bd close kapow-dtg
8. Clean up: delete the worktree branch

Be thorough but focused. Only change what the issue requires.

   109→	ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
   110→	defer cancel()
   111→
   112→	if cfg.DatabaseURL == "" {
   113→		return fmt.Errorf("kapow: DATABASE_URL is required")
   114→	}
   115→
   116→	pool, err := dbutil.Connect(ctx, cfg.DatabaseURL)
   117→	if err != nil {
   118→		return err
   119→	}
   120→	defer pool.Close()
   121→
   122→	fetcher := fetch.New(cfg.CacheDir)
   123→
   124→	if err := RunAll(ctx, cfg, pool, fetcher, skip, only); err != nil {
   125→		return err
   126→	}
   127→
   128→	slog.Info("refreshing materialized views")
   129→	return dbutil.RefreshMaterializedViews(ctx, pool)
   130→}
   131→
   132→func setupLogging(cfg *config.Config) {
   133→	level := cfg.SlogLevel()
   134→	handler := slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stderr, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: level})
   135→	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(handler))
   136→}
   137→
   138→func parseCSV(s string) map[string]bool {
   139→	m := make(map[string]bool)
   140→	if s == "" {
   141→		return m
   142→	}
   143→	for _, v := range strings.Split(s, ",") {
   144→		v = strings.TrimSpace(v)
   145→		if v != "" {
   146→			m[v] = true
   147→		}
   148→	}
   149→	return m
   150→}
   151→
   152→func printUsage() {
   153→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, `kapow — Kapow data import tool
   154→
   155→Usage: kapow <command> [flags]
   156→
   157→Commands:
   158→`)
   159→	for _, imp := range registry {
   160→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  %-16s %s\n", imp.Name(), imp.Description())
   161→	}
   162→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, `  %-16s Run all importers in dependency order
   163→  %-16s Print data source documentation
   164→
   165→Global flags:
   166→  --database-url   PostgreSQL DSN (default: $DATABASE_URL)
   167→  --cache-dir      Download cache dir (default: ~/.cache/kapow)
   168→  --log-level      debug|info|warn|error (default: info)
   169→  --dry-run        Parse and validate without writing to DB
   170→`, "run-all", "sources")
   171→}
   172→
   173→func printSources() {
   174→	fmt.Println("See DATA_SOURCES.md in this directory for complete data source documentation.")
   175→}
   176→

1→# Kapow Data Import Tool — Design Document
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→Kapow (College Power Search) has a fully defined PostgreSQL schema and a working
     6→API/frontend, but no data import pipeline. The database needs to be populated
     7→from government data (NCES College Scorecard, Clery Act crime stats), college
     8→ranking publications (US News, Forbes, Niche, etc.), athletic conference data
     9→(NCAA), climate data (Koppen), and other sources. This tool provides a single Go
    10→binary with subcommands to import all data sources, with download+cache support
    11→and proper documentation for sources that require web scraping.
    12→
    13→## Architecture
    14→
    15→### Module: `tools/importer/` (standalone Go module, like `tools/samplesql/`)
    16→
    17→```
    18→tools/importer/
    19→├── go.mod                       # github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/importer
    20→├── main.go                      # CLI entrypoint: subcommand dispatch
    21→├── registry.go                  # Importer registration table + run-all orchestrator
    22→├── DATA_SOURCES.md              # Data source URLs, scraping instructions, update cadence
    23→│
    24→├── internal/
    25→│   ├── config/config.go         # DATABASE_URL, CACHE_DIR, LOG_LEVEL, DRY_RUN
    26→│   ├── dbutil/
    27→│   │   ├── connect.go           # pgxpool connection with retry
    28→│   │   ├── upsert.go            # UpsertInstitution, UpsertDataset, UpsertAnnualDataset, MergeJSONB
    29→│   │   ├── batch.go             # CopyFrom bulk insert, BatchExec
    30→│   │   └── views.go             # RefreshMaterializedViews
    31→│   ├── fetch/
    32→│   │   ├── fetch.go             # HTTP download + ETag cache + decompression + local fallback
    33→│   │   └── fetch_test.go
    34→│   ├── csvutil/
    35→│   │   ├── reader.go            # Header-mapped CSV/TSV reader with typed column access
    36→│   │   └── reader_test.go
    37→│   ├── namematch/
    38→│   │   ├── matcher.go           # Multi-tier institution name matching
    39→│   │   ├── normalize.go         # Name canonicalization rules
    40→│   │   ├── aliases.go           # Hardcoded alias overrides (~1000 institution name mappings)
    41→│   │   ├── fuzzy.go             # Tier 2: Jaro-Winkler fuzzy matching (adrg/strutil)
    42→│   │   ├── llm.go               # Tier 3: LLM-assisted curation (Claude API, optional)
    43→│   │   └── matcher_test.go
    44→│   └── ranking/
    45→│       ├── builder.go           # Opinion type, RankingBuilder: accumulate → batch flush
    46→│       └── builder_test.go
    47→│
    48→├── nces/                        # NCES College Scorecard (institution foundation)
    49→│   ├── nces.go
    50→│   ├── fields.go                # CSV column → JSONB key mapping (200+ fields)
    51→│   └── nces_test.go
    52→├── ncesfos/                     # NCES Field of Study (CIP codes)
    53→├── rankings/                    # All 9+ ranking publications (one file each)
    54→│   ├── usnews.go, forbes.go, niche.go, timeshighered.go,
    55→│   │   qs.go, washmonthly.go, wsj.go, arwu.go, money.go
    56→│   └── rankings_test.go
    57→├── ncaa/                        # NCAA athletics
    58→│   ├── ncaa.go
    59→│   └── sports.go                # Sport→gender/category mapping
    60→├── weather/                     # Koppen climate zones
    61→├── crime/                       # Clery Act crime stats
    62→├── admissions/                  # Application platforms (Common App, Coalition, etc.)
    63→│   └── platforms.go             # Embedded platform membership lists
    64→├── misc/                        # Partisan lean, AAU, metro area
    65→├── zipcoords/                   # ZIP code coordinates
    66→└── normalizer/                  # institution_normalized_alias generator
    67→```
    68→
    69→### CLI Design
    70→
    71→Single binary, subcommand dispatch via stdlib `flag`:
    72→
    73→```
    74→kapow <command> [flags]
    75→
    76→Commands:
    77→  nces             Import NCES College Scorecard institutions
    78→  nces-fos         Import NCES Field of Study data
    79→  rankings         Import rankings (--publication=usnews|forbes|niche|...)
    80→  ncaa             Import NCAA athletics data
    81→  weather          Import Koppen climate classification
    82→  crime            Import Clery Act crime statistics
    83→  admissions       Import application platform membership
    84→  misc             Import misc data (partisan lean, AAU, metro area)
    85→  zipcoords        Import ZIP code coordinates
    86→  normalizer       Generate normalized institution aliases
    87→  run-all          Run all importers in dependency order
    88→  match-review     Review unmatched names from last run (fuzzy + LLM suggestions)
    89→  sources          Print data source documentation
    90→
    91→Global flags:
    92→  --database-url   PostgreSQL DSN (default: $DATABASE_URL)
    93→  --cache-dir      Download cache dir (default: ~/.cache/kapow)
    94→  --log-level      debug|info|warn|error (default: info)
    95→  --dry-run        Parse and validate without writing to DB
    96→
    97→Per-command flags:
    98→  --file PATH      Use local file instead of downloading
    99→  --year YYYY      Data year (where applicable)
   100→  rankings --publication SLUG [--category SLUG]
   101→  run-all --skip=weather,metro  |  --only=nces,rankings
   102→```
   103→
   104→### Importer Interface
   105→
   106→```go
   107→type Importer interface {
   108→    Name() string
   109→    Description() string
   110→    Dependencies() []string
   111→    RegisterFlags(fs *flag.FlagSet)
   112→    Run(ctx context.Context, cfg *Config, pool *pgxpool.Pool, fetcher *fetch.Fetcher) error
   113→}
   114→```
   115→
   116→### Shared Infrastructure
   117→
   118→**`internal/fetch`** — Download + cache. HTTP GET with ETag/Last-Modified
   119→conditional requests. Cache keyed by URL hash. Transparent .zip/.gz/.xz
   120→decompression. `--file` flag bypasses download. Stale cache preferred over
   121→failure when source is temporarily unavailable.
   122→
   123→**`internal/csvutil`** — Header-mapped CSV/TSV reader. `Row.String("COL")`,
   124→`Row.Int("COL")`, `Row.OptionalFloat("COL")` typed accessors. Avoids
   125→repetitive index-based column access.
   126→
   127→**`internal/namematch`** — Multi-tier institution name resolution:
   128→
   129→- **Tier 1 — Deterministic** (free, instant, ~95% of matches):
   130→  Loads all institutions + aliases from DB into memory maps. Exact name →
   131→  normalized name → alias lookup. Also supports OPE6/OPE8 ID lookups.
   132→  Normalization: lowercase, strip diacritics, remove "The ", strip punctuation,
   133→  collapse whitespace. Includes ~1000 hardcoded alias overrides for institutions
   134→  whose external names differ from their NCES canonical names.
   135→
   136→- **Tier 2 — Fuzzy string similarity** (free, fast, ~3-4% more):
   137→  Uses [adrg/strutil](https://github.com/adrg/strutil) for Jaro-Winkler
   138→  similarity. When Tier 1 fails, computes similarity against all institutions
   139→  (pre-filtered by state when available). Matches above threshold (0.92) are
   140→  flagged as candidates. Returned with confidence scores for human review.
   141→
   142→- **Tier 3 — LLM-assisted curation** (optional, pay-per-use, long tail):
   143→  For names that Tier 1+2 can't resolve. Batches unmatched names with context
   144→  (source publication, state, ranking position, top fuzzy candidates) and sends
   145→  to Claude API for suggested matches. Suggestions presented for human approval.
   146→
   147→  All approved matches (from Tier 2 or 3) are persisted as new aliases in
   148→  `search_base.institution_alias` for future deterministic matching.
   149→
   150→- **`match-review` subcommand**: Interactive review of unmatched names from the
   151→  last import run. Shows fuzzy candidates + LLM suggestions. `--approve` saves
   152→  selected matches as aliases.
   153→
   154→**`internal/ranking`** — `Opinion` type (rank/score/tier/range methods).
   155→`RankingBuilder` accumulates opinions and batch-flushes to `search_base.ranking`
   156→metadata + `search_base.annual_dataset` + `institution.rankings` JSONB. All 9
   157→ranking importers use this; their own code is just parse + match + derive opinion.
   158→
   159→**`internal/dbutil`** — Connection pool with retry. `UpsertInstitution` (core
   160→columns + JSONB merge). `MergeJSONB` (append to institution JSONB column without
   161→overwriting other importers' data). `UpsertDataset` / `UpsertAnnualDataset`.
   162→`RefreshMaterializedViews` (called once at end of run-all).
   163→
   164→### Pipeline Ordering (`run-all`)
   165→
   166→```
   167→Layer 0 (no deps):   nces, zipcoords
   168→Layer 1 (needs nces): nces-fos, rankings/*, ncaa, weather, crime,
   169→                       admissions, misc/*
   170→Layer 2 (needs all):  normalizer
   171→Final:                RefreshMaterializedViews
   172→```
   173→
   174→## Data Sources
   175→
   176→### Data Update Calendar
   177→
   178→Each source has an "expected" date (when it typically drops) and a "by" date
   179→(99.5% confidence it's out, without being a full year later).
   180→
   181→| Source | Expected Release | By Date | Notes |
   182→|--------|-----------------|---------|-------|
   183→| **College Scorecard** | Late April–May | Jun 30 | Apr 23 2025, May 6 2024. Oct update adds FSA data. |
   184→| **College Scorecard FOS** | Same as above | Jun 30 | Bundled with institution data release |
   185→| **US News** | Late Sep | Oct 15 | Sep 23 2025, Sep 2024. Always September. |
   186→| **Forbes** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 26 2025, Sep 7 2024. Late Aug–early Sep. |
   187→| **Niche** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 25 2025. Consistently late August. |
   188→| **Times Higher Ed** | Early–mid Oct | Nov 1 | Oct 9 2025, Oct 10 2024. Always October. |
   189→| **QS World** | Mid Jun | Jul 15 | Jun 19 2025, Jun 4 2024. Always June. |
   190→| **Washington Monthly** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 24 2025. Published with Sep/Oct issue. |
   191→| **WSJ** | Early Sep | Oct 1 | Sep 5 2024. Fall release. |
   192→| **ARWU/Shanghai** | Mid Aug | Sep 1 | Aug 15 2025. Consistently mid-August since 2003. |
   193→| **Money** | Late Jun | Jul 31 | Jun 25 2025. Summer release. |
   194→| **Crime/Clery** | Oct 1 deadline | Nov 15 | Institutions must publish by Oct 1; DOE data files appear weeks later. |
   195→| **NCAA Directory** | Aug (academic year) | Sep 30 | Updated for each academic year. |
   196→| **Partisan Lean** | Post-election | Mar (odd years) | FiveThirtyEight updates after each election cycle. |
   197→| **ZIP Coordinates** | Stable | N/A | GeoNames data; updated continuously but rarely changes. |
   198→| **Koppen Climate** | Stable | N/A | Climate classifications; updated every ~10 years. |
   199→
   200→**Suggested annual import schedule:**
   201→1. **May–Jun**: College Scorecard + FOS (foundation refresh), QS, Money
   202→2. **Aug–Sep**: ARWU, Forbes, Niche, Washington Monthly, NCAA, US News, WSJ
   203→3. **Oct–Nov**: Times Higher Ed, Crime/Clery
   204→4. **After each run**: normalizer, materialized view refresh
   205→
   206→### Programmatic Download (automatable)
   207→
   208→| Source | URL / API | Format |
   209→|--------|-----------|--------|
   210→| **College Scorecard** | `https://collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/` — bulk CSV download | CSV (ZIP) |
   211→| **College Scorecard FOS** | Same site, FieldOfStudyData files | CSV (ZIP) |
   212→| **Forbes Rankings** | `https://www.forbes.com/forbesapi/org/top-colleges/{year}/position/true.json?limit=1000&fields=...` | JSON |
   213→| **Niche Rankings** | `https://www.niche.com/api/renaissance/results/?type=private&type=public&listURL=best-colleges&page={i}&searchType=college` (109 pages, needs User-Agent + Referer headers, 200s sleep between requests) | JSON |
   214→| **Crime Data** | `https://ope.ed.gov/campussafety/#/datafile/list` — downloadable XLS, convert to CSV via libreoffice | XLS→CSV |
   215→| **Partisan Lean** | FiveThirtyEight CSV (GitHub) | CSV |
   216→| **ZIP Coordinates** | Public domain CSV (GeoNames or similar) | CSV |
   217→| **Koppen Climate** | Public domain ASCII grid | Text |
   218→| **PayScale** | `https://www.payscale.com/college-roi/page/2` — extract from page props JSON | HTML→JSON |
   219→
   220→### Web Scraping Required (semi-automated with docs)
   221→
   222→| Source | Website | Method | Notes |
   223→|--------|---------|--------|-------|
   224→| **ARWU/Shanghai** | `shanghairanking.com/rankings/arwu/{year}` | Scrape HTML table or download PDF | No API. PDF at `shanghairanking.com/_pub/com/arwu/{year}/...pdf`. Use goquery for HTML or manual PDF download. |
   225→| **NCAA Sports** | `web3.ncaa.org/directory/memberList` | Scrape or manual export | NCAA Directory has member listings by division. May need browser automation or manual CSV export. |
   226→| **Money Magazine** | `money.com/best-colleges/` | Scrape HTML table | Extract `tr[data-id]` rows, parse `.stars-score` and `.col-name` via goquery |
   227→| **Princeton Review** | princetonreview.com | Scrape HTML | Category-based lists (most liberal, most conservative, etc.) |
   228→| **Times Higher Ed** | `timeshighereducation.com/world-university-rankings/{year}` | JSON API behind the page, or scrape | May need browser dev tools to find the API endpoint |
   229→| **QS World** | QS website | Previously available as TSV/CSV | Check if still downloadable or needs scraping |
   230→| **Washington Monthly** | washingtonmonthly.com | JSON or scrape | Check current availability |
   231→| **WSJ Rankings** | WSJ website | JSON embedded in page | `{ colleges: [...] }` structure |
   232→
   233→### Pre-curated / Aggregated Sources
   234→
   235→| Source | Location | Notes |
   236→|--------|----------|-------|
   237→| **US News Historical** | [frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings](https://github.com/frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings) | CSV archive 1984–2024. Free for public use. |
   238→| **Multi-ranking aggregate** | [frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data](https://github.com/frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data) | SAT, acceptance rate, US News/THE/QS/CSRankings |
   239→| **AAU Membership** | Embedded list (~71 institutions) | Stable; update when new members added |
   240→| **Common App / Coalition / etc.** | Embedded lists | Update annually from organization websites |
   241→
   242→### Approach for Non-Downloadable Sources
   243→
   244→For sources that require scraping, the importer will:
   245→1. **Try automated download first** (known API/URL patterns)
   246→2. **Fall back to cached local file** if download fails
   247→3. **Document manual steps** in `DATA_SOURCES.md` with exact commands
   248→4. **Accept `--file` flag** so user can provide manually-obtained data
   249→
   250→For scrapers specifically:
   251→- Use `net/http` + `github.com/PuerkitoBio/goquery` for HTML table extraction
   252→- Use `chromedp` only if JavaScript rendering is required
   253→- Keep scraping code isolated in each importer package for easy updates
   254→
   255→## Key Files to Reference
   256→
   257→- `tools/samplesql/main.go` — CLI pattern, flag parsing, DB connection
   258→- `tools/samplesql/extractor.go` — pgx query patterns, column detection
   259→- `api/internal/db/db.go` — Connection with retry pattern
   260→- `api/migrations/00001_initial_schema.sql` — Complete target schema
   261→- `api/internal/search/registry.go` — JSONB paths the API queries (consumer contract)
   262→
   263→## Issue Breakdown
   264→
   265→### Epic: Data Import Tool (`tools/importer`)
   266→
   267→---
   268→
   269→#### 1. Scaffold importer module
   270→- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
   271→- **Deps**: none
   272→- **Description**: Create `tools/importer/` Go module with `go.mod`
   273→  (`github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/importer`), `main.go` with subcommand
   274→  dispatch via stdlib `flag`, `internal/config/config.go` (DATABASE_URL,
   275→  CACHE_DIR, LOG_LEVEL, DRY_RUN flags + env fallback). Include `Importer`
   276→  interface definition in `registry.go`. Compile and run `kapow --help`.
   277→- **Done when**: `go build ./...` succeeds, `kapow --help` prints usage.
   278→
   279→#### 2. Implement internal/dbutil
   280→- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
   281→- **Deps**: 1
   282→- **Description**: `internal/dbutil/` package: `connect.go` (pgxpool with retry,
   283→  reuse pattern from `api/internal/db/db.go`), `upsert.go` (UpsertInstitution
   284→  with JSONB merge via `COALESCE(col,'{}'::jsonb) || $N`, UpsertDataset,
   285→  UpsertAnnualDataset, MergeJSONB), `batch.go` (CopyFrom wrapper, BatchExec
   286→  using pgx.Batch), `views.go` (RefreshMaterializedViews for dataset_merged +
   287→  dataset_latest). Include Institution/Dataset/AnnualDataset Go structs.
   288→  Integration tests against dev DB.
   289→- **Done when**: Integration test creates/updates an institution + dataset + refreshes views.
   290→
   291→#### 3. Implement internal/fetch
   292→- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
   293→- **Deps**: 1
   294→- **Description**: `internal/fetch/` package: HTTP GET with ETag/Last-Modified
   295→  conditional requests, cache keyed by SHA-256 of URL under `~/.cache/kapow/`,
   296→  transparent .zip/.gz/.xz decompression via `GetReader()`, `--file` local
   297→  override bypass, stale-cache-on-failure resilience. Unit tests with
   298→  `httptest.Server`.
   299→- **Done when**: Unit tests pass for cache hit, miss, conditional 304, decompression, local fallback.
   300→
   301→#### 4. Implement internal/csvutil
   302→- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
   303→- **Deps**: 1
   304→- **Description**: `internal/csvutil/` package: header-mapped CSV/TSV reader
   305→  wrapping `encoding/csv`. `NewReader(r, opts...)` with `WithDelimiter`,
   306→  `WithLazyQuotes`. `Row` type with `String(col)`, `Int(col)`, `Float(col)`,
   307→  `OptionalString(col)`, `OptionalInt(col)`, `OptionalFloat(col)`. Handle
   308→  missing columns gracefully (return zero/nil). Unit tests with embedded
   309→  test CSV data.
   310→- **Done when**: Unit tests cover normal parsing, TSV, missing columns, type coercion, quoted values.
   311→
   312→#### 5. Implement internal/namematch (Tier 1 — deterministic)
   313→- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
   314→- **Deps**: 2
   315→- **Description**: `internal/namematch/` package. `matcher.go`: Load all
   316→  institutions + aliases from DB into memory maps. 3-tier lookup: exact
   317→  lowercased name → normalized name → alias. Also `MatchByOPE6`/`MatchByOPE8`.
   318→  `normalize.go`: lowercase, strip diacritics (x/text/transform), remove "The ",
   319→  strip punctuation, collapse whitespace. `aliases.go`: ~1000 hardcoded alias
   320→  overrides for institutions whose external names differ from NCES canonical
   321→  names (e.g. "Arizona State University" → "Arizona State University - Tempe").
   322→  Unit tests for normalization; integration test for full matcher load + lookup.
   323→- **Done when**: Unit tests pass; integration test resolves known aliases → correct unit_id.
   324→
   325→#### 6. Implement NCES College Scorecard importer
   326→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P0
   327→- **Deps**: 2, 3, 4
   328→- **Description**: `nces/` package. Downloads College Scorecard CSV from
   329→  `collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/`. `fields.go`: maps 200+ CSV columns to JSONB
   330→  keys (demographics, test scores, costs, admissions, programs). `nces.go`:
   331→  parses CSV, filters (skip non-degree-granting, graduate-only, missing OPE IDs),
   332→  handles OPE6/OPE8 deduplication (prefer lower unit_id), inserts states into
   333→  `location.state`, creates institutions with coordinates + nces JSONB, creates
   334→  CORE and ACADEMICS datasets. Unit tests with 10-row fixture CSV; integration
   335→  test against dev DB.
   336→- **Done when**: Integration test imports fixture → institutions + datasets exist in DB with correct JSONB.
   337→
   338→#### 7. Implement internal/ranking builder
   339→- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P1
   340→- **Deps**: 2, 5
   341→- **Description**: `internal/ranking/` package. `Opinion` struct (publication/
   342→  category slugs, method rank/score/tier/range, year, unit_id, rank, score, tier,
   343→  rank range). `Builder` accumulates opinions, `Flush(ctx)` batch-writes:
   344→  upsert ranking metadata into `search_base.ranking`, upsert into
   345→  `search_base.annual_dataset` (theme=RANKING), merge into
   346→  `institution.rankings` JSONB. Implement `deriveOpinion()` to parse numeric
   347→  ranks, tier strings ("Tier 1"), and ranges ("201-300"). Unit tests for opinion
   348→  derivation; integration test for flush.
   349→- **Done when**: Integration test flushes 10 opinions → ranking + annual_dataset + institution.rankings populated.
   350→
   351→#### 8. Implement NCES Field of Study importer
   352→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
   353→- **Deps**: 6
   354→- **Description**: `ncesfos/` package. Parses FieldOfStudyData CSV. Maps
   355→  credential level codes (1=CERT, 2=ASSOC, 3=BACH, 4=MAST, 5=DOCT, 6=PROF).
   356→  Groups by unit_id + CIP code. Writes to `institution.fields_of_study` JSONB
   357→  and inserts into `search_base.cip_data`. Uses canonicalUnitId() for OPE
   358→  normalization. Unit test with fixture; integration test.
   359→- **Done when**: cip_data table populated, institution.fields_of_study has grouped CIP data.
   360→
   361→#### 9. Implement US News ranking importer
   362→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
   363→- **Deps**: 7
   364→- **Description**: `rankings/usnews.go`. Parses historical CSV (1984–2024, from
   365→  frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings or local file). Handles numeric
   366→  ranks and tier strings. Multiple categories (National Universities, Liberal
   367→  Arts). Uses name matcher. Feeds RankingBuilder. Unit test with fixture rows.
   368→- **Done when**: Rankings for US News categories appear in institution.rankings JSONB.
   369→
   370→#### 10. Implement NCAA athletics importer
   371→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
   372→- **Deps**: 5, 6
   373→- **Description**: `ncaa/` package. `sports.go`: maps 38 sports to gender
   374→  (men/women/coed), football subdivision detection (FBS/FCS). `ncaa.go`: parses
   375→  CSV, 3-tier name matching, inserts conferences into `search_base.conference`,
   376→  writes `institution.athletics` JSONB + ATHLETICS dataset. Document NCAA
   377→  Directory as data source (manual CSV export from web3.ncaa.org or scrape).
   378→  Unit + integration tests.
   379→- **Done when**: conference table populated, athletics JSONB has division/conference/sports.
   380→
   381→#### 11. Implement Weather/Koppen importer
   382→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
   383→- **Deps**: 6
   384→- **Description**: `weather/` package. Parses Koppen-Geiger ASCII text file
   385→  (lat, lon, classification). `grid.go`: normalizes institution coordinates to
   386→  0.25/0.75 grid, spiral search up to 3 degrees for nearest match. Writes
   387→  `institution.weather` JSONB + WEATHER dataset. Unit test for grid/spiral logic;
   388→  integration test.
   389→- **Done when**: institution.weather has Koppen classification for all institutions with coordinates.
   390→
   391→#### 12. Implement ZIP coordinates importer
   392→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
   393→- **Deps**: 2, 4
   394→- **Description**: `zipcoords/` package. Parses CSV (ZIP, lat, lon, municipality,
   395→  state). Bulk inserts into `location.zip_coordinates` with PostGIS geometry
   396→  (`ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(lon,lat),4326)`). Uses CopyFrom for performance.
   397→  Unit test for CSV parsing; integration test.
   398→- **Done when**: zip_coordinates table populated with ~42,000 rows.
   399→
   400→#### 13. Implement Forbes ranking importer
   401→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   402→- **Deps**: 7
   403→- **Description**: `rankings/forbes.go`. Downloads from Forbes API JSON endpoint.
   404→  Parses organization name + rank. Uses name matcher → RankingBuilder. Unit test.
   405→- **Done when**: Forbes rankings in institution.rankings JSONB.
   406→
   407→#### 14. Implement Niche ranking importer
   408→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   409→- **Deps**: 7, 3
   410→- **Description**: `rankings/niche.go`. Paginated API scrape (109 pages, needs
   411→  User-Agent + Referer headers, 200s sleep between requests). Parses JSON
   412→  entities with IPEDS IDs (direct match, no name matching needed). Feeds
   413→  RankingBuilder. Document scraping procedure in DATA_SOURCES.md.
   414→- **Done when**: Niche rankings imported; scraping documented.
   415→
   416→#### 15. Implement Times Higher Ed ranking importer
   417→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   418→- **Deps**: 7
   419→- **Description**: `rankings/timeshighered.go`. Parses JSON. Handles numeric
   420→  ranks and tier bands ("400-500"). Includes overall score. Filters "Reporter"
   421→  entries. Name matching via aliases. Unit test.
   422→- **Done when**: THE rankings in institution.rankings JSONB.
   423→
   424→#### 16. Implement QS World ranking importer
   425→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   426→- **Deps**: 7, 4
   427→- **Description**: `rankings/qs.go`. Parses TSV. Handles tied ranks (trailing
   428→  '='). Multiple years per file. Overall score included. Unit test.
   429→- **Done when**: QS rankings imported for multiple years.
   430→
   431→#### 17. Implement Washington Monthly ranking importer
   432→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   433→- **Deps**: 7
   434→- **Description**: `rankings/washmonthly.go`. Parses JSON. Category-based
   435→  (national universities, liberal arts). Uses unit_id from JSON when available,
   436→  name matching as fallback. Unit test.
   437→- **Done when**: WashMonthly rankings in JSONB by category.
   438→
   439→#### 18. Implement WSJ ranking importer
   440→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   441→- **Deps**: 7
   442→- **Description**: `rankings/wsj.go`. Parses JSON `{ colleges: [...] }` with
   443→  unitid + rank. Direct unit_id matching + name fallback. Unit test.
   444→- **Done when**: WSJ rankings in institution.rankings JSONB.
   445→
   446→#### 19. Implement ARWU/Shanghai ranking importer
   447→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   448→- **Deps**: 7, 3
   449→- **Description**: `rankings/arwu.go`. Scrapes ranking table from
   450→  `shanghairanking.com/rankings/arwu/{year}` using goquery or downloads PDF.
   451→  Parses name + national rank + total score. Name matching. Document scraping
   452→  procedure. Also support local JSON file for pre-scraped data.
   453→- **Done when**: ARWU rankings imported; scraping documented in DATA_SOURCES.md.
   454→
   455→#### 20. Implement Money ranking importer
   456→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   457→- **Deps**: 7
   458→- **Description**: `rankings/money.go`. Embedded data for current year.
   459→  Also document HTML scraping procedure for future updates (goquery on
   460→  `money.com/best-colleges/`, extract `tr[data-id]`). Unit test.
   461→- **Done when**: Money rankings imported from embedded data.
   462→
   463→#### 21. Implement Crime/Clery Act importer
   464→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   465→- **Deps**: 6, 4
   466→- **Description**: `crime/` package. Parses multiple CSVs (on-campus crime,
   467→  non-campus crime, on-campus VAWA, non-campus VAWA — converted from XLS via
   468→  libreoffice). Deduplicates by OPE8 preferring Main Campus. Aggregates crime
   469→  categories (murder, rape, robbery, etc.) and VAWA categories (dating violence,
   470→  stalking). Writes `institution.safety_and_security` JSONB + SAFETY dataset.
   471→  Document XLS→CSV conversion step. Unit + integration tests.
   472→- **Done when**: safety_and_security JSONB populated with crime + VAWA counts.
   473→
   474→#### 22. Implement Admissions importer
   475→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   476→- **Deps**: 5, 6
   477→- **Description**: `admissions/` package. `platforms.go`: embedded membership
   478→  lists for Common App (~1500), Coalition (~150), ApplyTexas, Apply SUNY, UC
   479→  system, CSU system. `admissions.go`: determines platform membership by
   480→  name/alias matching + prefix detection (UC/CSU). Writes
   481→  `institution.admissions` JSONB + ADMISSIONS dataset. Unit + integration tests.
   482→- **Done when**: admissions JSONB has application platform flags for matched institutions.
   483→
   484→#### 23. Implement Misc importers (partisan lean, AAU, metro)
   485→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
   486→- **Deps**: 6
   487→- **Description**: `misc/` package. Three sub-importers:
   488→  `partisan.go` — parses FiveThirtyEight CSV, PostGIS spatial join to
   489→  congressional districts. `aau.go` — embedded list of ~71 AAU members, name
   490→  matching. `metro.go` — CBSA/metro area lookup via PostGIS spatial query.
   491→  All write to `institution.misc` JSONB + CULTURAL_ENVIRONMENT dataset.
   492→  Unit tests for parsing; integration test for PostGIS joins.
   493→- **Done when**: misc JSONB has partisan_lean, aau_member, metro_area fields.
   494→
   495→#### 24. Implement Name Normalizer
   496→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
   497→- **Deps**: 5, 6 (ideally all importers complete, but not a hard dep)
   498→- **Description**: `normalizer/` package. Reads all institutions from DB, runs
   499→  `namematch.Normalize(name)`, detects collisions, writes to
   500→  `location.institution_normalized_alias` (unit_id, normalized_alias, city,
   501→  state_code). Should run last in the pipeline. Unit + integration tests.
   502→- **Done when**: institution_normalized_alias table populated for all institutions.
   503→
   504→#### 25. Implement run-all pipeline
   505→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
   506→- **Deps**: all importers (6–24)
   507→- **Description**: `run-all` subcommand in `registry.go`. Executes importers in
   508→  dependency order (Layer 0: nces + zipcoords → Layer 1: all others → Layer 2:
   509→  normalizer → Final: RefreshMaterializedViews). Supports `--skip=weather,metro`
   510→  and `--only=nces,rankings` flags. Logs per-importer timing and summary stats.
   511→  Stops on first error (with `--continue-on-error` flag to override).
   512→- **Done when**: `kapow run-all` populates a fresh DB end-to-end.
   513→
   514→#### 26. Implement namematch Tier 2 (fuzzy matching)
   515→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
   516→- **Deps**: 5
   517→- **Description**: `internal/namematch/fuzzy.go`. Uses `adrg/strutil` for
   518→  Jaro-Winkler similarity. When Tier 1 fails, computes similarity against all
   519→  institutions (pre-filtered by state when available from source data). Returns
   520→  top candidates above threshold (0.92) with confidence scores. Logs unmatched
   521→  names + fuzzy candidates to a JSON report file for review.
   522→- **Done when**: Unit test shows fuzzy match for misspelled institution name; report file generated.
   523→
   524→#### 27. Implement match-review subcommand
   525→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P4
   526→- **Deps**: 26
   527→- **Description**: `match-review` subcommand. Loads unmatched name report from
   528→  last import run. Displays each unmatched name with top fuzzy candidates.
   529→  `--approve ID` persists a match as a new alias in `search_base.institution_alias`.
   530→  `--reject ID` marks as known non-match (skip in future runs). Tracks review
   531→  state in a local JSON file.
   532→- **Done when**: Can review unmatched names interactively and persist aliases.
   533→
   534→#### 28. Implement namematch Tier 3 (LLM-assisted)
   535→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P4
   536→- **Deps**: 27
   537→- **Description**: `internal/namematch/llm.go`. `--llm-assist` flag on
   538→  match-review. Batches unmatched names with context (source, state, ranking,
   539→  fuzzy candidates) and sends to Claude API. Presents LLM suggestions alongside
   540→  fuzzy candidates in match-review. Uses `anthropic-sdk-go`. All matches still
   541→  require human approval.
   542→- **Done when**: LLM suggestions appear in match-review for unresolved names.
   543→
   544→#### 29. Write DATA_SOURCES.md
   545→- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P3
   546→- **Deps**: 6 (enough context to write; update as importers are added)
   547→- **Description**: `tools/importer/DATA_SOURCES.md`. Complete documentation of
   548→  every data source: URL, access method (download/API/scrape), file format,
   549→  update cadence with expected + by dates, manual steps where automation isn't
   550→  possible (XLS→CSV conversion, NCAA directory export, ARWU scraping commands).
   551→  Include the annual import schedule.
   552→- **Done when**: Every data source documented with actionable instructions.
   553→
   554→#### 30. Update Makefile and documentation
   555→- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P3
   556→- **Deps**: 1
   557→- **Description**: Add to root Makefile: `kapow-build` (compiles binary),
   558→  `kapow-test` (runs tests), `kapow-test-unit` (short tests). Update
   559→  `tools/CLAUDE.md` with kapow tool documentation. Update root `CLAUDE.md` if
   560→  needed (add kapow tool to "Common task recipes").
   561→- **Done when**: `make kapow-build` and `make kapow-test-unit` work from root.
   562→
   563→---
   564→
   565→### Dependency Graph
   566→
   567→```
   568→1 (scaffold)
   569→├── 2 (dbutil) ──┬── 5 (namematch) ──┬── 7 (ranking builder) ──┬── 9 (usnews)
   570→│                │                   │                         ├── 13 (forbes)
   571→│                │                   │                         ├── 14 (niche)
   572→│                │                   │                         ├── 15 (THE)
   573→│                │                   │                         ├── 16 (qs)
   574→│                │                   │                         ├── 17 (washmonthly)
   575→│                │                   │                         ├── 18 (wsj)
   576→│                │                   │                         ├── 19 (arwu)
   577→│                │                   │                         └── 20 (money)
   578→│                │                   ├── 10 (ncaa)
   579→│                │                   ├── 22 (admissions)
   580→│                │                   └── 26 (fuzzy) → 27 (match-review) → 28 (llm)
   581→│                │
   582→│                ├── 6 (nces) ───────┬── 8 (nces-fos)
   583→│                │                   ├── 10 (ncaa) [also needs 5]
   584→│                │                   ├── 11 (weather)
   585→│                │                   ├── 21 (crime)
   586→│                │                   ├── 22 (admissions) [also needs 5]
   587→│                │                   ├── 23 (misc)
   588→│                │                   └── 24 (normalizer) [also needs 5]
   589→│                │
   590→│                └── 12 (zipcoords)
   591→│
   592→├── 3 (fetch) ───── 6 (nces), 14 (niche), 19 (arwu)
   593→├── 4 (csvutil) ─── 6 (nces), 12 (zipcoords), 16 (qs), 21 (crime)
   594→└── 30 (makefile)
   595→
   596→25 (run-all) depends on all importers
   597→29 (DATA_SOURCES.md) depends on 6 (enough to start writing)
   598→```
   599→
   600→## Verification
   601→
   602→1. `cd tools/importer && go build ./...` — compiles
   603→2. `cd tools/importer && go test -short ./...` — unit tests pass (no DB)
   604→3. `cd tools/importer && go test -run Integration ./...` — integration tests pass (with DB)
   605→4. `kapow nces --file <scorecard.csv>` — imports institutions into dev DB
   606→5. `kapow run-all` — full pipeline completes, all tables populated
   607→6. `curl http://localhost:8081/private/healthcheck` — API works against imported data
   608→7. Verify search returns results via GraphQL query

file.content:
# Kapow Data Import Tool — Design Document

## Context

Kapow (College Power Search) has a fully defined PostgreSQL schema and a working
API/frontend, but no data import pipeline. The database needs to be populated
from government data (NCES College Scorecard, Clery Act crime stats), college
ranking publications (US News, Forbes, Niche, etc.), athletic conference data
(NCAA), climate data (Koppen), and other sources. This tool provides a single Go
binary with subcommands to import all data sources, with download+cache support
and proper documentation for sources that require web scraping.

## Architecture

### Module: `tools/importer/` (standalone Go module, like `tools/samplesql/`)

```
tools/importer/
├── go.mod                       # github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/importer
├── main.go                      # CLI entrypoint: subcommand dispatch
├── registry.go                  # Importer registration table + run-all orchestrator
├── DATA_SOURCES.md              # Data source URLs, scraping instructions, update cadence
│
├── internal/
│   ├── config/config.go         # DATABASE_URL, CACHE_DIR, LOG_LEVEL, DRY_RUN
│   ├── dbutil/
│   │   ├── connect.go           # pgxpool connection with retry
│   │   ├── upsert.go            # UpsertInstitution, UpsertDataset, UpsertAnnualDataset, MergeJSONB
│   │   ├── batch.go             # CopyFrom bulk insert, BatchExec
│   │   └── views.go             # RefreshMaterializedViews
│   ├── fetch/
│   │   ├── fetch.go             # HTTP download + ETag cache + decompression + local fallback
│   │   └── fetch_test.go
│   ├── csvutil/
│   │   ├── reader.go            # Header-mapped CSV/TSV reader with typed column access
│   │   └── reader_test.go
│   ├── namematch/
│   │   ├── matcher.go           # Multi-tier institution name matching
│   │   ├── normalize.go         # Name canonicalization rules
│   │   ├── aliases.go           # Hardcoded alias overrides (~1000 institution name mappings)
│   │   ├── fuzzy.go             # Tier 2: Jaro-Winkler fuzzy matching (adrg/strutil)
│   │   ├── llm.go               # Tier 3: LLM-assisted curation (Claude API, optional)
│   │   └── matcher_test.go
│   └── ranking/
│       ├── builder.go           # Opinion type, RankingBuilder: accumulate → batch flush
│       └── builder_test.go
│
├── nces/                        # NCES College Scorecard (institution foundation)
│   ├── nces.go
│   ├── fields.go                # CSV column → JSONB key mapping (200+ fields)
│   └── nces_test.go
├── ncesfos/                     # NCES Field of Study (CIP codes)
├── rankings/                    # All 9+ ranking publications (one file each)
│   ├── usnews.go, forbes.go, niche.go, timeshighered.go,
│   │   qs.go, washmonthly.go, wsj.go, arwu.go, money.go
│   └── rankings_test.go
├── ncaa/                        # NCAA athletics
│   ├── ncaa.go
│   └── sports.go                # Sport→gender/category mapping
├── weather/                     # Koppen climate zones
├── crime/                       # Clery Act crime stats
├── admissions/                  # Application platforms (Common App, Coalition, etc.)
│   └── platforms.go             # Embedded platform membership lists
├── misc/                        # Partisan lean, AAU, metro area
├── zipcoords/                   # ZIP code coordinates
└── normalizer/                  # institution_normalized_alias generator
```

### CLI Design

Single binary, subcommand dispatch via stdlib `flag`:

```
kapow <command> [flags]

Commands:
  nces             Import NCES College Scorecard institutions
  nces-fos         Import NCES Field of Study data
  rankings         Import rankings (--publication=usnews|forbes|niche|...)
  ncaa             Import NCAA athletics data
  weather          Import Koppen climate classification
  crime            Import Clery Act crime statistics
  admissions       Import application platform membership
  misc             Import misc data (partisan lean, AAU, metro area)
  zipcoords        Import ZIP code coordinates
  normalizer       Generate normalized institution aliases
  run-all          Run all importers in dependency order
  match-review     Review unmatched names from last run (fuzzy + LLM suggestions)
  sources          Print data source documentation

Global flags:
  --database-url   PostgreSQL DSN (default: $DATABASE_URL)
  --cache-dir      Download cache dir (default: ~/.cache/kapow)
  --log-level      debug|info|warn|error (default: info)
  --dry-run        Parse and validate without writing to DB

Per-command flags:
  --file PATH      Use local file instead of downloading
  --year YYYY      Data year (where applicable)
  rankings --publication SLUG [--category SLUG]
  run-all --skip=weather,metro  |  --only=nces,rankings
```

### Importer Interface

```go
type Importer interface {
    Name() string
    Description() string
    Dependencies() []string
    RegisterFlags(fs *flag.FlagSet)
    Run(ctx context.Context, cfg *Config, pool *pgxpool.Pool, fetcher *fetch.Fetcher) error
}
```

### Shared Infrastructure

**`internal/fetch`** — Download + cache. HTTP GET with ETag/Last-Modified
conditional requests. Cache keyed by URL hash. Transparent .zip/.gz/.xz
decompression. `--file` flag bypasses download. Stale cache preferred over
failure when source is temporarily unavailable.

**`internal/csvutil`** — Header-mapped CSV/TSV reader. `Row.String("COL")`,
`Row.Int("COL")`, `Row.OptionalFloat("COL")` typed accessors. Avoids
repetitive index-based column access.

**`internal/namematch`** — Multi-tier institution name resolution:

- **Tier 1 — Deterministic** (free, instant, ~95% of matches):
  Loads all institutions + aliases from DB into memory maps. Exact name →
  normalized name → alias lookup. Also supports OPE6/OPE8 ID lookups.
  Normalization: lowercase, strip diacritics, remove "The ", strip punctuation,
  collapse whitespace. Includes ~1000 hardcoded alias overrides for institutions
  whose external names differ from their NCES canonical names.

- **Tier 2 — Fuzzy string similarity** (free, fast, ~3-4% more):
  Uses [adrg/strutil](https://github.com/adrg/strutil) for Jaro-Winkler
  similarity. When Tier 1 fails, computes similarity against all institutions
  (pre-filtered by state when available). Matches above threshold (0.92) are
  flagged as candidates. Returned with confidence scores for human review.

- **Tier 3 — LLM-assisted curation** (optional, pay-per-use, long tail):
  For names that Tier 1+2 can't resolve. Batches unmatched names with context
  (source publication, state, ranking position, top fuzzy candidates) and sends
  to Claude API for suggested matches. Suggestions presented for human approval.

  All approved matches (from Tier 2 or 3) are persisted as new aliases in
  `search_base.institution_alias` for future deterministic matching.

- **`match-review` subcommand**: Interactive review of unmatched names from the
  last import run. Shows fuzzy candidates + LLM suggestions. `--approve` saves
  selected matches as aliases.

**`internal/ranking`** — `Opinion` type (rank/score/tier/range methods).
`RankingBuilder` accumulates opinions and batch-flushes to `search_base.ranking`
metadata + `search_base.annual_dataset` + `institution.rankings` JSONB. All 9
ranking importers use this; their own code is just parse + match + derive opinion.

**`internal/dbutil`** — Connection pool with retry. `UpsertInstitution` (core
columns + JSONB merge). `MergeJSONB` (append to institution JSONB column without
overwriting other importers' data). `UpsertDataset` / `UpsertAnnualDataset`.
`RefreshMaterializedViews` (called once at end of run-all).

### Pipeline Ordering (`run-all`)

```
Layer 0 (no deps):   nces, zipcoords
Layer 1 (needs nces): nces-fos, rankings/*, ncaa, weather, crime,
                       admissions, misc/*
Layer 2 (needs all):  normalizer
Final:                RefreshMaterializedViews
```

## Data Sources

### Data Update Calendar

Each source has an "expected" date (when it typically drops) and a "by" date
(99.5% confidence it's out, without being a full year later).

| Source | Expected Release | By Date | Notes |
|--------|-----------------|---------|-------|
| **College Scorecard** | Late April–May | Jun 30 | Apr 23 2025, May 6 2024. Oct update adds FSA data. |
| **College Scorecard FOS** | Same as above | Jun 30 | Bundled with institution data release |
| **US News** | Late Sep | Oct 15 | Sep 23 2025, Sep 2024. Always September. |
| **Forbes** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 26 2025, Sep 7 2024. Late Aug–early Sep. |
| **Niche** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 25 2025. Consistently late August. |
| **Times Higher Ed** | Early–mid Oct | Nov 1 | Oct 9 2025, Oct 10 2024. Always October. |
| **QS World** | Mid Jun | Jul 15 | Jun 19 2025, Jun 4 2024. Always June. |
| **Washington Monthly** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 24 2025. Published with Sep/Oct issue. |
| **WSJ** | Early Sep | Oct 1 | Sep 5 2024. Fall release. |
| **ARWU/Shanghai** | Mid Aug | Sep 1 | Aug 15 2025. Consistently mid-August since 2003. |
| **Money** | Late Jun | Jul 31 | Jun 25 2025. Summer release. |
| **Crime/Clery** | Oct 1 deadline | Nov 15 | Institutions must publish by Oct 1; DOE data files appear weeks later. |
| **NCAA Directory** | Aug (academic year) | Sep 30 | Updated for each academic year. |
| **Partisan Lean** | Post-election | Mar (odd years) | FiveThirtyEight updates after each election cycle. |
| **ZIP Coordinates** | Stable | N/A | GeoNames data; updated continuously but rarely changes. |
| **Koppen Climate** | Stable | N/A | Climate classifications; updated every ~10 years. |

**Suggested annual import schedule:**
1. **May–Jun**: College Scorecard + FOS (foundation refresh), QS, Money
2. **Aug–Sep**: ARWU, Forbes, Niche, Washington Monthly, NCAA, US News, WSJ
3. **Oct–Nov**: Times Higher Ed, Crime/Clery
4. **After each run**: normalizer, materialized view refresh

### Programmatic Download (automatable)

| Source | URL / API | Format |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| **College Scorecard** | `https://collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/` — bulk CSV download | CSV (ZIP) |
| **College Scorecard FOS** | Same site, FieldOfStudyData files | CSV (ZIP) |
| **Forbes Rankings** | `https://www.forbes.com/forbesapi/org/top-colleges/{year}/position/true.json?limit=1000&fields=...` | JSON |
| **Niche Rankings** | `https://www.niche.com/api/renaissance/results/?type=private&type=public&listURL=best-colleges&page={i}&searchType=college` (109 pages, needs User-Agent + Referer headers, 200s sleep between requests) | JSON |
| **Crime Data** | `https://ope.ed.gov/campussafety/#/datafile/list` — downloadable XLS, convert to CSV via libreoffice | XLS→CSV |
| **Partisan Lean** | FiveThirtyEight CSV (GitHub) | CSV |
| **ZIP Coordinates** | Public domain CSV (GeoNames or similar) | CSV |
| **Koppen Climate** | Public domain ASCII grid | Text |
| **PayScale** | `https://www.payscale.com/college-roi/page/2` — extract from page props JSON | HTML→JSON |

### Web Scraping Required (semi-automated with docs)

| Source | Website | Method | Notes |
|--------|---------|--------|-------|
| **ARWU/Shanghai** | `shanghairanking.com/rankings/arwu/{year}` | Scrape HTML table or download PDF | No API. PDF at `shanghairanking.com/_pub/com/arwu/{year}/...pdf`. Use goquery for HTML or manual PDF download. |
| **NCAA Sports** | `web3.ncaa.org/directory/memberList` | Scrape or manual export | NCAA Directory has member listings by division. May need browser automation or manual CSV export. |
| **Money Magazine** | `money.com/best-colleges/` | Scrape HTML table | Extract `tr[data-id]` rows, parse `.stars-score` and `.col-name` via goquery |
| **Princeton Review** | princetonreview.com | Scrape HTML | Category-based lists (most liberal, most conservative, etc.) |
| **Times Higher Ed** | `timeshighereducation.com/world-university-rankings/{year}` | JSON API behind the page, or scrape | May need browser dev tools to find the API endpoint |
| **QS World** | QS website | Previously available as TSV/CSV | Check if still downloadable or needs scraping |
| **Washington Monthly** | washingtonmonthly.com | JSON or scrape | Check current availability |
| **WSJ Rankings** | WSJ website | JSON embedded in page | `{ colleges: [...] }` structure |

### Pre-curated / Aggregated Sources

| Source | Location | Notes |
|--------|----------|-------|
| **US News Historical** | [frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings](https://github.com/frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings) | CSV archive 1984–2024. Free for public use. |
| **Multi-ranking aggregate** | [frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data](https://github.com/frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data) | SAT, acceptance rate, US News/THE/QS/CSRankings |
| **AAU Membership** | Embedded list (~71 institutions) | Stable; update when new members added |
| **Common App / Coalition / etc.** | Embedded lists | Update annually from organization websites |

### Approach for Non-Downloadable Sources

For sources that require scraping, the importer will:
1. **Try automated download first** (known API/URL patterns)
2. **Fall back to cached local file** if download fails
3. **Document manual steps** in `DATA_SOURCES.md` with exact commands
4. **Accept `--file` flag** so user can provide manually-obtained data

For scrapers specifically:
- Use `net/http` + `github.com/PuerkitoBio/goquery` for HTML table extraction
- Use `chromedp` only if JavaScript rendering is required
- Keep scraping code isolated in each importer package for easy updates

## Key Files to Reference

- `tools/samplesql/main.go` — CLI pattern, flag parsing, DB connection
- `tools/samplesql/extractor.go` — pgx query patterns, column detection
- `api/internal/db/db.go` — Connection with retry pattern
- `api/migrations/00001_initial_schema.sql` — Complete target schema
- `api/internal/search/registry.go` — JSONB paths the API queries (consumer contract)

## Issue Breakdown

### Epic: Data Import Tool (`tools/importer`)

---

#### 1. Scaffold importer module
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: none
- **Description**: Create `tools/importer/` Go module with `go.mod`
  (`github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/importer`), `main.go` with subcommand
  dispatch via stdlib `flag`, `internal/config/config.go` (DATABASE_URL,
  CACHE_DIR, LOG_LEVEL, DRY_RUN flags + env fallback). Include `Importer`
  interface definition in `registry.go`. Compile and run `kapow --help`.
- **Done when**: `go build ./...` succeeds, `kapow --help` prints usage.

#### 2. Implement internal/dbutil
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: 1
- **Description**: `internal/dbutil/` package: `connect.go` (pgxpool with retry,
  reuse pattern from `api/internal/db/db.go`), `upsert.go` (UpsertInstitution
  with JSONB merge via `COALESCE(col,'{}'::jsonb) || $N`, UpsertDataset,
  UpsertAnnualDataset, MergeJSONB), `batch.go` (CopyFrom wrapper, BatchExec
  using pgx.Batch), `views.go` (RefreshMaterializedViews for dataset_merged +
  dataset_latest). Include Institution/Dataset/AnnualDataset Go structs.
  Integration tests against dev DB.
- **Done when**: Integration test creates/updates an institution + dataset + refreshes views.

#### 3. Implement internal/fetch
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: 1
- **Description**: `internal/fetch/` package: HTTP GET with ETag/Last-Modified
  conditional requests, cache keyed by SHA-256 of URL under `~/.cache/kapow/`,
  transparent .zip/.gz/.xz decompression via `GetReader()`, `--file` local
  override bypass, stale-cache-on-failure resilience. Unit tests with
  `httptest.Server`.
- **Done when**: Unit tests pass for cache hit, miss, conditional 304, decompression, local fallback.

#### 4. Implement internal/csvutil
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: 1
- **Description**: `internal/csvutil/` package: header-mapped CSV/TSV reader
  wrapping `encoding/csv`. `NewReader(r, opts...)` with `WithDelimiter`,
  `WithLazyQuotes`. `Row` type with `String(col)`, `Int(col)`, `Float(col)`,
  `OptionalString(col)`, `OptionalInt(col)`, `OptionalFloat(col)`. Handle
  missing columns gracefully (return zero/nil). Unit tests with embedded
  test CSV data.
- **Done when**: Unit tests cover normal parsing, TSV, missing columns, type coercion, quoted values.

#### 5. Implement internal/namematch (Tier 1 — deterministic)
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: 2
- **Description**: `internal/namematch/` package. `matcher.go`: Load all
  institutions + aliases from DB into memory maps. 3-tier lookup: exact
  lowercased name → normalized name → alias. Also `MatchByOPE6`/`MatchByOPE8`.
  `normalize.go`: lowercase, strip diacritics (x/text/transform), remove "The ",
  strip punctuation, collapse whitespace. `aliases.go`: ~1000 hardcoded alias
  overrides for institutions whose external names differ from NCES canonical
  names (e.g. "Arizona State University" → "Arizona State University - Tempe").
  Unit tests for normalization; integration test for full matcher load + lookup.
- **Done when**: Unit tests pass; integration test resolves known aliases → correct unit_id.

#### 6. Implement NCES College Scorecard importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: 2, 3, 4
- **Description**: `nces/` package. Downloads College Scorecard CSV from
  `collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/`. `fields.go`: maps 200+ CSV columns to JSONB
  keys (demographics, test scores, costs, admissions, programs). `nces.go`:
  parses CSV, filters (skip non-degree-granting, graduate-only, missing OPE IDs),
  handles OPE6/OPE8 deduplication (prefer lower unit_id), inserts states into
  `location.state`, creates institutions with coordinates + nces JSONB, creates
  CORE and ACADEMICS datasets. Unit tests with 10-row fixture CSV; integration
  test against dev DB.
- **Done when**: Integration test imports fixture → institutions + datasets exist in DB with correct JSONB.

#### 7. Implement internal/ranking builder
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 2, 5
- **Description**: `internal/ranking/` package. `Opinion` struct (publication/
  category slugs, method rank/score/tier/range, year, unit_id, rank, score, tier,
  rank range). `Builder` accumulates opinions, `Flush(ctx)` batch-writes:
  upsert ranking metadata into `search_base.ranking`, upsert into
  `search_base.annual_dataset` (theme=RANKING), merge into
  `institution.rankings` JSONB. Implement `deriveOpinion()` to parse numeric
  ranks, tier strings ("Tier 1"), and ranges ("201-300"). Unit tests for opinion
  derivation; integration test for flush.
- **Done when**: Integration test flushes 10 opinions → ranking + annual_dataset + institution.rankings populated.

#### 8. Implement NCES Field of Study importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 6
- **Description**: `ncesfos/` package. Parses FieldOfStudyData CSV. Maps
  credential level codes (1=CERT, 2=ASSOC, 3=BACH, 4=MAST, 5=DOCT, 6=PROF).
  Groups by unit_id + CIP code. Writes to `institution.fields_of_study` JSONB
  and inserts into `search_base.cip_data`. Uses canonicalUnitId() for OPE
  normalization. Unit test with fixture; integration test.
- **Done when**: cip_data table populated, institution.fields_of_study has grouped CIP data.

#### 9. Implement US News ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/usnews.go`. Parses historical CSV (1984–2024, from
  frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings or local file). Handles numeric
  ranks and tier strings. Multiple categories (National Universities, Liberal
  Arts). Uses name matcher. Feeds RankingBuilder. Unit test with fixture rows.
- **Done when**: Rankings for US News categories appear in institution.rankings JSONB.

#### 10. Implement NCAA athletics importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 5, 6
- **Description**: `ncaa/` package. `sports.go`: maps 38 sports to gender
  (men/women/coed), football subdivision detection (FBS/FCS). `ncaa.go`: parses
  CSV, 3-tier name matching, inserts conferences into `search_base.conference`,
  writes `institution.athletics` JSONB + ATHLETICS dataset. Document NCAA
  Directory as data source (manual CSV export from web3.ncaa.org or scrape).
  Unit + integration tests.
- **Done when**: conference table populated, athletics JSONB has division/conference/sports.

#### 11. Implement Weather/Koppen importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 6
- **Description**: `weather/` package. Parses Koppen-Geiger ASCII text file
  (lat, lon, classification). `grid.go`: normalizes institution coordinates to
  0.25/0.75 grid, spiral search up to 3 degrees for nearest match. Writes
  `institution.weather` JSONB + WEATHER dataset. Unit test for grid/spiral logic;
  integration test.
- **Done when**: institution.weather has Koppen classification for all institutions with coordinates.

#### 12. Implement ZIP coordinates importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 2, 4
- **Description**: `zipcoords/` package. Parses CSV (ZIP, lat, lon, municipality,
  state). Bulk inserts into `location.zip_coordinates` with PostGIS geometry
  (`ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(lon,lat),4326)`). Uses CopyFrom for performance.
  Unit test for CSV parsing; integration test.
- **Done when**: zip_coordinates table populated with ~42,000 rows.

#### 13. Implement Forbes ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/forbes.go`. Downloads from Forbes API JSON endpoint.
  Parses organization name + rank. Uses name matcher → RankingBuilder. Unit test.
- **Done when**: Forbes rankings in institution.rankings JSONB.

#### 14. Implement Niche ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7, 3
- **Description**: `rankings/niche.go`. Paginated API scrape (109 pages, needs
  User-Agent + Referer headers, 200s sleep between requests). Parses JSON
  entities with IPEDS IDs (direct match, no name matching needed). Feeds
  RankingBuilder. Document scraping procedure in DATA_SOURCES.md.
- **Done when**: Niche rankings imported; scraping documented.

#### 15. Implement Times Higher Ed ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/timeshighered.go`. Parses JSON. Handles numeric
  ranks and tier bands ("400-500"). Includes overall score. Filters "Reporter"
  entries. Name matching via aliases. Unit test.
- **Done when**: THE rankings in institution.rankings JSONB.

#### 16. Implement QS World ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7, 4
- **Description**: `rankings/qs.go`. Parses TSV. Handles tied ranks (trailing
  '='). Multiple years per file. Overall score included. Unit test.
- **Done when**: QS rankings imported for multiple years.

#### 17. Implement Washington Monthly ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/washmonthly.go`. Parses JSON. Category-based
  (national universities, liberal arts). Uses unit_id from JSON when available,
  name matching as fallback. Unit test.
- **Done when**: WashMonthly rankings in JSONB by category.

#### 18. Implement WSJ ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/wsj.go`. Parses JSON `{ colleges: [...] }` with
  unitid + rank. Direct unit_id matching + name fallback. Unit test.
- **Done when**: WSJ rankings in institution.rankings JSONB.

#### 19. Implement ARWU/Shanghai ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7, 3
- **Description**: `rankings/arwu.go`. Scrapes ranking table from
  `shanghairanking.com/rankings/arwu/{year}` using goquery or downloads PDF.
  Parses name + national rank + total score. Name matching. Document scraping
  procedure. Also support local JSON file for pre-scraped data.
- **Done when**: ARWU rankings imported; scraping documented in DATA_SOURCES.md.

#### 20. Implement Money ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/money.go`. Embedded data for current year.
  Also document HTML scraping procedure for future updates (goquery on
  `money.com/best-colleges/`, extract `tr[data-id]`). Unit test.
- **Done when**: Money rankings imported from embedded data.

#### 21. Implement Crime/Clery Act importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 6, 4
- **Description**: `crime/` package. Parses multiple CSVs (on-campus crime,
  non-campus crime, on-campus VAWA, non-campus VAWA — converted from XLS via
  libreoffice). Deduplicates by OPE8 preferring Main Campus. Aggregates crime
  categories (murder, rape, robbery, etc.) and VAWA categories (dating violence,
  stalking). Writes `institution.safety_and_security` JSONB + SAFETY dataset.
  Document XLS→CSV conversion step. Unit + integration tests.
- **Done when**: safety_and_security JSONB populated with crime + VAWA counts.

#### 22. Implement Admissions importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 5, 6
- **Description**: `admissions/` package. `platforms.go`: embedded membership
  lists for Common App (~1500), Coalition (~150), ApplyTexas, Apply SUNY, UC
  system, CSU system. `admissions.go`: determines platform membership by
  name/alias matching + prefix detection (UC/CSU). Writes
  `institution.admissions` JSONB + ADMISSIONS dataset. Unit + integration tests.
- **Done when**: admissions JSONB has application platform flags for matched institutions.

#### 23. Implement Misc importers (partisan lean, AAU, metro)
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: 6
- **Description**: `misc/` package. Three sub-importers:
  `partisan.go` — parses FiveThirtyEight CSV, PostGIS spatial join to
  congressional districts. `aau.go` — embedded list of ~71 AAU members, name
  matching. `metro.go` — CBSA/metro area lookup via PostGIS spatial query.
  All write to `institution.misc` JSONB + CULTURAL_ENVIRONMENT dataset.
  Unit tests for parsing; integration test for PostGIS joins.
- **Done when**: misc JSONB has partisan_lean, aau_member, metro_area fields.

#### 24. Implement Name Normalizer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: 5, 6 (ideally all importers complete, but not a hard dep)
- **Description**: `normalizer/` package. Reads all institutions from DB, runs
  `namematch.Normalize(name)`, detects collisions, writes to
  `location.institution_normalized_alias` (unit_id, normalized_alias, city,
  state_code). Should run last in the pipeline. Unit + integration tests.
- **Done when**: institution_normalized_alias table populated for all institutions.

#### 25. Implement run-all pipeline
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: all importers (6–24)
- **Description**: `run-all` subcommand in `registry.go`. Executes importers in
  dependency order (Layer 0: nces + zipcoords → Layer 1: all others → Layer 2:
  normalizer → Final: RefreshMaterializedViews). Supports `--skip=weather,metro`
  and `--only=nces,rankings` flags. Logs per-importer timing and summary stats.
  Stops on first error (with `--continue-on-error` flag to override).
- **Done when**: `kapow run-all` populates a fresh DB end-to-end.

#### 26. Implement namematch Tier 2 (fuzzy matching)
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: 5
- **Description**: `internal/namematch/fuzzy.go`. Uses `adrg/strutil` for
  Jaro-Winkler similarity. When Tier 1 fails, computes similarity against all
  institutions (pre-filtered by state when available from source data). Returns
  top candidates above threshold (0.92) with confidence scores. Logs unmatched
  names + fuzzy candidates to a JSON report file for review.
- **Done when**: Unit test shows fuzzy match for misspelled institution name; report file generated.

#### 27. Implement match-review subcommand
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P4
- **Deps**: 26
- **Description**: `match-review` subcommand. Loads unmatched name report from
  last import run. Displays each unmatched name with top fuzzy candidates.
  `--approve ID` persists a match as a new alias in `search_base.institution_alias`.
  `--reject ID` marks as known non-match (skip in future runs). Tracks review
  state in a local JSON file.
- **Done when**: Can review unmatched names interactively and persist aliases.

#### 28. Implement namematch Tier 3 (LLM-assisted)
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P4
- **Deps**: 27
- **Description**: `internal/namematch/llm.go`. `--llm-assist` flag on
  match-review. Batches unmatched names with context (source, state, ranking,
  fuzzy candidates) and sends to Claude API. Presents LLM suggestions alongside
  fuzzy candidates in match-review. Uses `anthropic-sdk-go`. All matches still
  require human approval.
- **Done when**: LLM suggestions appear in match-review for unresolved names.

#### 29. Write DATA_SOURCES.md
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: 6 (enough context to write; update as importers are added)
- **Description**: `tools/importer/DATA_SOURCES.md`. Complete documentation of
  every data source: URL, access method (download/API/scrape), file format,
  update cadence with expected + by dates, manual steps where automation isn't
  possible (XLS→CSV conversion, NCAA directory export, ARWU scraping commands).
  Include the annual import schedule.
- **Done when**: Every data source documented with actionable instructions.

#### 30. Update Makefile and documentation
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: 1
- **Description**: Add to root Makefile: `kapow-build` (compiles binary),
  `kapow-test` (runs tests), `kapow-test-unit` (short tests). Update
  `tools/CLAUDE.md` with kapow tool documentation. Update root `CLAUDE.md` if
  needed (add kapow tool to "Common task recipes").
- **Done when**: `make kapow-build` and `make kapow-test-unit` work from root.

---

### Dependency Graph

```
1 (scaffold)
├── 2 (dbutil) ──┬── 5 (namematch) ──┬── 7 (ranking builder) ──┬── 9 (usnews)
│                │                   │                         ├── 13 (forbes)
│                │                   │                         ├── 14 (niche)
│                │                   │                         ├── 15 (THE)
│                │                   │                         ├── 16 (qs)
│                │                   │                         ├── 17 (washmonthly)
│                │                   │                         ├── 18 (wsj)
│                │                   │                         ├── 19 (arwu)
│                │                   │                         └── 20 (money)
│                │                   ├── 10 (ncaa)
│                │                   ├── 22 (admissions)
│                │                   └── 26 (fuzzy) → 27 (match-review) → 28 (llm)
│                │
│                ├── 6 (nces) ───────┬── 8 (nces-fos)
│                │                   ├── 10 (ncaa) [also needs 5]
│                │                   ├── 11 (weather)
│                │                   ├── 21 (crime)
│                │                   ├── 22 (admissions) [also needs 5]
│                │                   ├── 23 (misc)
│                │                   └── 24 (normalizer) [also needs 5]
│                │
│                └── 12 (zipcoords)
│
├── 3 (fetch) ───── 6 (nces), 14 (niche), 19 (arwu)
├── 4 (csvutil) ─── 6 (nces), 12 (zipcoords), 16 (qs), 21 (crime)
└── 30 (makefile)

25 (run-all) depends on all importers
29 (DATA_SOURCES.md) depends on 6 (enough to start writing)
```

## Verification

1. `cd tools/importer && go build ./...` — compiles
2. `cd tools/importer && go test -short ./...` — unit tests pass (no DB)
3. `cd tools/importer && go test -run Integration ./...` — integration tests pass (with DB)
4. `kapow nces --file <scorecard.csv>` — imports institutions into dev DB
5. `kapow run-all` — full pipeline completes, all tables populated
6. `curl http://localhost:8081/private/healthcheck` — API works against imported data
7. Verify search returns results via GraphQL query
